title: Dynamic Entanglement Distribution for Multi-User and Multi-Protocol Quantum Networking
headline: Dynamic Entanglement Distribution Enables Metropolitan-Scale Quantum Networking
tldr: The study demonstrates a quantum reconfigurable optical add-drop multiplexer (q-ROADM) that dynamically routes polarization-entangled photon pairs to six users across a campus. This approach showcases scalable, multi-user quantum communication over existing fiber infrastructure.
significance: It provides a practical, reconfigurable solution for expanding quantum networks to real-world metropolitan settings.

title: Relativistic time-commutative dynamics with $κ$-plane noncommutativity
headline: κ-Plane Noncommutativity in Relativistic Dynamics
tldr: The paper investigates time‑commutative dynamics on a κ‑deformed plane, deriving modified relativistic equations and exploring their implications for quantum‑gravity phenomenology.
significance: It provides a novel framework connecting noncommutative geometry to potentially observable relativistic effects.

title: Long-range and steady-state entanglement of driven-dissipative nitrogen vacancy centers using microwaves as a drive and synthetic antiferromagnet as a dissipator
headline: Microwave-Driven NV Centers Achieve Long-Range Steady-State Entanglement via Synthetic Antiferromagnet
tldr: Researchers demonstrate that microwaves can drive nitrogen-vacancy centers into a long-range, steady-state entangled configuration using a synthetic antiferromagnet as a dissipative environment. This approach offers a scalable pathway for solid-state quantum networks and sensing applications.
significance: This work provides a practical route to stabilize entanglement between distant solid-state qubits, a key hurdle for quantum computing and high-precision sensing.

title: Decoding Market Emotion from Blockchain Activity: A Data-Driven Sentiment Classifier
headline: Using Blockchain Data and Social Media to Classify Bitcoin Market Sentiment
tldr: This study introduces a sentiment classifier that fuses on-chain activity, financial indicators, and social media posts to explain Bitcoin market emotions. Unlike price prediction models, it focuses on interpreting market sentiment rather than forecasting prices.
significance: By linking blockchain transactions with public sentiment, the work offers a novel lens for understanding crypto market dynamics beyond price movements.

title: SearchOS-V1: Towards Robust Open-Domain Information-Seeking Agent Collaboration
headline: SearchOS‑V1: Enhancing Robust Open‑Domain Agent Collaboration for Efficient Web Search
tldr: SearchOS‑V1 introduces a framework that improves task tracking for web‑searching agents, preventing repetitive loops when evidence is scarce. By integrating multi‑agent coordination and adaptive search budgets, it achieves more efficient information retrieval in open‑domain settings.
significance: This work addresses a critical bottleneck in large‑language‑model‑powered agents, enabling more reliable and resource‑efficient web search.

title: HoloGeo: Mitigating Landmark Bias in Geo-localization via Evidence-Driven Reasoning
headline: HoloGeo: Reducing Landmark Bias in Image Geo-Localization with Evidence-Driven Reasoning
tldr: HoloGeo introduces quantitative metrics Bias Intensity and Bias Harmfulness to assess landmark bias in vision-language models for geo-localization. By incorporating evidence-driven reasoning, the approach significantly improves accuracy and reduces spurious correlations.
significance: This work provides the first systematic framework to quantify and mitigate landmark bias, advancing reliable geo-localization for real-world applications.

title: teLLMe Why (Ain't Nothing but a Jam): Exploratory Causal Analysis of Urban Driving Data
headline: Exploring Causal Effects in Urban Driving Data with teLLMe System
tldr: Traffic agencies now have vast video-derived data but lack causal insights. teLLMe offers an exploratory framework to answer questions like how rain affects traffic density.
significance: This approach enables data-driven policy decisions by revealing causal relationships in urban mobility.

title: Bridge Evidence: Static Retrieval Utility Does Not Predict Causal Utility in Multi-Step Agentic Search
headline: Static Retrieval Scores Fail for Multi-Step Agentic Search in LLMs
tldr: Traditional retrieval evaluation treats documents as static, but when an LLM acts as a search agent across multiple turns, the usefulness of a document changes. This study shows that static utility scores do not predict causal utility in multi-step search, highlighting the need for dynamic evaluation benchmarks.
significance: The findings reveal that current retrieval benchmarks misrepresent document usefulness for agentic LLMs, urging the development of more realistic evaluation frameworks.

title: Altermagnetic spin textures coupled to superconductors: Domain wall spin-triplet superconductivity and supercurrent-induced torques
headline: Altermagnetic Domain Walls Induce Spin-Triplet Superconductivity and Current-Driven Torques in Altermagnets
tldr: We investigate how spatially varying altermagnetic textures affect Cooper pairing in s-wave superconductors, revealing that altermagnetism suppresses spin-singlet pairing and promotes local spin-triplet correlations near domain walls. These triplet states enable supercurrent-induced torques, offering a new route to manipulate magnetic textures via superconducting currents.
significance: This work uncovers a novel mechanism for generating spin-triplet superconductivity and current-driven magnetic torques, potentially enabling low-dissipation spintronic devices.

title: ARMOR++: Agentic Orchestration of a Multi-Domain Primitive Set for Transferable Attacks on Deepfake Detectors
headline: ARMOR++: Transferable Adversarial Attacks Against Deepfake Detectors via Multi-Domain Primitives
tldr: ARMOR++ demonstrates that adversarial perturbations crafted on convolutional surrogates can be transferred to transformer-based deepfake detectors, even under strict no-query constraints, by orchestrating a multi-domain primitive set that preserves semantic awareness. The approach reveals the fragility of current forensic cues and offers a benchmark for evaluating detector robustness.
significance: This work exposes the vulnerability of state-of-the-art deepfake detectors and provides a transferable attack framework that can guide future defense research.

title: When will T Coronae Borealis next erupt as a nova? Constraints from recurrence, orbital phase, and accretion-state evolution
headline: Predicting T Coronae Borealis' Next Nova: Recurrence, Orbital Phase, and Accretion Dynamics
tldr: Scientists combine historical eruption intervals, orbital timing, and recent accretion changes to estimate a 30% chance of T CrB erupting before 2026 July 11. The analysis refines the expected window for the next nova outburst.
significance: This refined timing informs targeted monitoring and enhances understanding of recurrent nova mechanisms.

title: Hierarchical Denoising For Multi-Step Visual Reasoning
headline: Hierarchical Denoising Enhances Multi-Step Visual Reasoning in Streaming Diffusion Models
tldr: This work introduces a hierarchical denoising framework that balances efficient streaming inference with bidirectional global revision, enabling logical consistency in complex visual reasoning tasks. The method reduces inference latency while maintaining high-quality predictions, addressing key limitations of current diffusion-based video models.
significance: It advances the capability of vision foundation models to perform human-like multi-step reasoning in real-time settings.

title: Partition, Prompt, Aggregate: Statistical Self-Consistency in Language Models
headline: Ensuring Statistical Self-Consistency in Language Models: A Law of Total Probability Test
tldr: This paper examines whether large language models treat prompts as conditional distributions by testing the law of total probability. The authors find that while models often approximate these identities, systematic deviations reveal limits of in‑context learning.
significance: The study provides a principled framework for evaluating the probabilistic soundness of in‑context learning, guiding future model design.

title: Locality of deep thermalisation through the lens of entanglement teleportation
headline: Locality of Deep Thermalisation Revealed via Entanglement Teleportation in Disconnected Subsystems
tldr: The study shows that deep thermalisation can occur locally even when subsystems remain causally disconnected. This challenges the assumption that global measurements are necessary for universal state ensembles.
significance: It provides new insights into how quantum information propagates and thermalises without requiring direct interaction, with implications for quantum computing and simulation.

title: RoboTTT: Context Scaling for Robot Policies
headline: RoboTTT: Scaling Robot Visuomotor Context to 8K Timesteps Without Latency
tldr: RoboTTT extends robot foundation models by training policies that maintain an 8,000‑step visual history, three orders of magnitude longer than current systems, while keeping inference speed unchanged. This extended context unlocks one‑shot in‑context learning and other advanced capabilities.
significance: By dramatically increasing context length without latency penalties, RoboTTT enables robots to perform complex, long‑horizon tasks that were previously infeasible.

title: MeanFlowNFT: Bringing Forward-Process RL to Average-Velocity Generators
headline: MeanFlowNFT: RL-Enhanced Fast Sampling for Diffusion Models
tldr: MeanFlowNFT combines MeanFlow generators, which predict average velocities for rapid few-step sampling, with DiffusionNFT's forward-process reinforcement learning to align diffusion outputs with human preferences. The resulting framework achieves efficient, high-quality generation without the need for reverse diffusion steps.
significance: It demonstrates a scalable, RL-driven approach to accelerate diffusion-based generation while maintaining alignment with user intent.

title: SciDiagramEdit: Learning to Edit Scientific Diagrams from Paper Revisions
headline: Automating Scientific Figure Editing via Natural Language for Paper Revisions
tldr: The paper introduces SciDiagramEdit, a system that learns to modify scientific diagrams based on natural language instructions derived from paper revision histories. It demonstrates that combining large language models with diffusion-based image editing can accurately relabel, rearrange, and restyle diagram components, streamlining the revision workflow.
significance: By enabling automated, instruction-driven editing of dense scientific figures, SciDiagramEdit reduces manual effort and improves reproducibility in scientific publishing.

title: Online Neural Space Time Memory for Dynamic Novel View Synthesis
headline: Real-Time Online Memory for Dynamic Novel View Synthesis from Streaming Videos
tldr: This work introduces an online neural space-time memory system that updates via Test‑Time Training, enabling real‑time novel view synthesis from multi‑view video streams while preserving long‑horizon memory of occluded regions. The method balances memory persistence with strict real‑time constraints, offering a practical solution for dynamic scene reconstruction.
significance: It demonstrates how Test‑Time Training can be leveraged for continuous, real‑time view synthesis, bridging the gap between memory‑rich reconstruction and low‑latency performance.

title: Motion-Conditioned Multi-View Fusion for Myocardial Infarction Localization from Echocardiography
headline: Motion‑Conditioned Multi‑View Fusion for Real‑Time Myocardial Infarction Localization in Echo
tldr: This study introduces a motion‑conditioned multi‑view fusion framework that automatically localizes myocardial infarction regions in echocardiography, reducing the need for extensive manual annotations. The method leverages temporal dynamics and view‑specific features to achieve accurate, real‑time detection.
significance: By enabling automated, annotation‑efficient MI localization, the approach could accelerate clinical decision‑making and improve patient outcomes.

title: Pretraining Data Can Be Poisoned through Computational Propaganda
headline: Computational Propaganda Threatens LLMs: Poisoning Pretraining Data Across Multiple Large-Scale Corpora
tldr: This study demonstrates how computational propaganda can poison large language model pretraining data, embedding harmful behaviors that are hard to detect. It highlights gaps in prior research that focused on limited sources like Wikipedia and shows the interplay between poisoned data and curation pipelines.
significance: Understanding data poisoning in pretraining is crucial for safeguarding the integrity of future foundation models.

title: Tunable Mpemba effect in a polymer-bead system with inertia
headline: Experimentally Motivated Model Controls Mpemba Effect via Inertia in Polymer-Bead Systems
tldr: We present a model where a polymer bead’s denaturation plateau slows relaxation, allowing the Mpemba effect to be tuned by inertia. By preparing the system at higher temperature or weaker force, the bead gains kinetic energy, accelerating its cooling relative to a colder start.
significance: This work demonstrates that inertia can be harnessed to control anomalous cooling, offering new insights into non-equilibrium thermodynamics and potential applications in smart material design.

title: SceneBind: Binding What and Where Across Vision, Audio and Language
headline: SceneBind: Omni-Modal Representation Linking What and Where Across Vision, Audio, and Language
tldr: SceneBind introduces an omni-modal framework that jointly models semantic content and 3D spatial structure across vision, audio, and language modalities. By representing scenes as semantic-spatial entities, it overcomes the limitations of prior encoders that focus solely on instance-level semantics.
significance: It enables richer contextual understanding for multimodal AI, paving the way for more accurate scene interpretation and spatial reasoning.

title: Products of simplices are canonically Ramsey
headline: Canonical Ramsey Property of Simplices
tldr: The paper proves that products of simplices are canonically Ramsey, extending known results for triangles to higher-dimensional simplicial products.
significance: It identifies a broad new family of geometric configurations that must appear either monochromatically or rainbowly under any finite coloring.

title: Beyond Success Rate: Cost-Aware Evaluation of Offensive and Defensive Security Agents
headline: Measuring Security Agents by Cost, Not Just Success: A New Evaluation Framework
tldr: Traditional security agent benchmarks focus on peak success rates, ignoring resource consumption. We propose a cost-aware evaluation that accounts for reasoning steps, tool calls, and telemetry queries, providing a more realistic measure of operational performance.
significance: This approach enables more accurate assessment of security agents for real-world deployment by balancing effectiveness with resource efficiency.

title: Geospatial & AI Trends: Highlights from 2025 - Geoawesome
headline: 2025 GeoAI Trends: Predictive Insights, Automation, and Climate Solutions for Cities and Global Challenges
tldr: 2025 saw GeoAI harnessing satellite imagery, LLMs, and real‑time analytics to forecast climate events and optimize urban planning. These advances enable faster decision‑making for disaster response, resource allocation, and sustainable development.
significance: These trends empower governments and businesses to proactively address environmental risks and build resilient infrastructure.

title: Towards the next generation of Geospatial Artificial Intelligence
headline: Charting the Future of GeoAI: Emerging Trends and Next-Gen Innovations
tldr: GeoAI merges geospatial science with AI, rapidly evolving as a leading research frontier. The paper reviews recent breakthroughs and outlines future directions for the field.
significance: Understanding GeoAI's trajectory is essential for harnessing AI to solve complex spatial challenges.

title: GeoAI | Geo Week News
headline: AI Drives GeoAI Revolution: Automated Feature Extraction, Point Cloud Classification, and Predictive Geospatial Analytics
tldr: Artificial intelligence is transforming geospatial workflows by automating feature extraction and classifying point clouds, enabling faster, more accurate analysis. These advances unlock predictive insights across the entire data pipeline, from imagery to spatial datasets.
significance: GeoAI's integration of AI models with large-scale geospatial data is accelerating decision-making in fields like urban planning, agriculture, and disaster response.

title: ACMSIGSPATIAL-GEOAI 2025 - Conference Tagline
headline: Transforming Geospatial Science with AI: Deep Learning, Multimodal Data, and Accelerated Architectures
tldr: Artificial intelligence breakthroughs, coupled with hardware accelerators and advanced data processing, are reshaping geospatial information science. The conference explores deep learning techniques that automatically extract features from massive multimodal datasets, driving solutions for societal challenges.
significance: This conference showcases how AI-driven methodologies are unlocking new insights and applications across geospatial domains, accelerating progress toward addressing global challenges.

title: Top 5 Geospatial AI Trends to Watch in 2025 - arizton.com
headline: Top 5 GeoAI Trends to Watch in 2025: From Foundation Models to Real-Time Urban Insights
tldr: GeoAI is rapidly evolving, and 2025 will see breakthroughs in foundation models, remote sensing, and real-time urban analytics. These trends promise smarter agriculture, flood forecasting, and autonomous logistics.
significance: Understanding these trends equips planners, farmers, and defense analysts to leverage AI for smarter, data-driven decisions.

title: GitHub - mbzuai-oryx/ThinkGeo: [CVPR 2026 ] ThinkGeo is a ...
headline: ThinkGeo: Benchmarking LLMs on Complex Remote Sensing Tasks with Structured Reasoning
tldr: ThinkGeo is a new benchmark that tests language model agents on remote sensing queries requiring step‑by‑step reasoning and tool use. It uses human‑curated satellite and aerial imagery questions across domains like disaster response, urban planning, and agriculture.
significance: It provides a rigorous, real‑world evaluation of LLMs’ spatial reasoning and tool‑driven capabilities in remote sensing.

title: remote-sensing · GitHub Topics · GitHub
headline: Remote Sensing Change Detection: Datasets, Tools, Methods, and Competitions Guide
tldr: This repository compiles the latest datasets, tools, and research papers for detecting changes in remote sensing imagery. It also lists active competitions and provides a curated bibliography for researchers and practitioners.
significance: It serves as a central hub for advancing remote sensing change detection research and applications.

title: GeoAI: Artificial Intelligence for Geospatial Data - GeoAI
headline: GeoAI: Bridging AI and Geospatial Analysis with an Intuitive Python Toolkit
tldr: GeoAI is a Python package that integrates machine learning with geospatial data, enabling researchers to apply AI techniques to geographic datasets. Its user-friendly API simplifies tasks such as segmentation, object detection, and change detection across satellite imagery and point clouds.
significance: GeoAI democratizes advanced AI methods for geospatial analysis, accelerating insights in fields from urban planning to environmental monitoring.

title: SkyTruth
headline: SkyTruth: Open-Source Satellite Tools Reveal Hidden Human Impact on the Planet
tldr: SkyTruth develops open-source software that analyzes satellite imagery to uncover environmental changes. Their advanced analytics expose hidden human activities, providing actionable insights for conservation.
significance: By making powerful remote sensing tools freely available, SkyTruth empowers researchers and policymakers to detect and address environmental threats worldwide.

title: Raster Vision: An open-source machine learning library for deep ...
headline: Raster Vision: Open-Source Deep Learning Toolkit Bridging GIS and Computer Vision
tldr: Raster Vision is an open-source library that integrates GIS data with deep learning pipelines for tasks like chip classification, semantic segmentation, and object detection. It offers a configurable workflow, making it accessible for researchers and developers working on remote sensing imagery.
significance: By unifying GIS and computer vision, Raster Vision accelerates the development of spatial AI solutions across agriculture, urban planning, and environmental monitoring.

title: NASA Releases Galileo: The Open-Source Multimodal Model Advancing Earth ...
headline: NASA Unveils Galileo: Open-Source Multimodal Model for Earth Observation Platform
tldr: NASA has released Galileo, an open-source multimodal foundation model that processes satellite imagery, time series, and tabular data in a single architecture. Its tokenization pipeline splits remote sensing inputs into spatial patches, timesteps, and logical channel groups, enabling versatile Earth observation tasks.
significance: Galileo democratizes advanced Earth observation analytics by providing a unified, open-source framework that can handle diverse data types, accelerating research and operational applications.

title: Sail membranes for optomechanical accelerometry
headline: Sail-Like Membrane Resonators Achieve Low Frequency and High Q-Mass for Optomechanical Accelerometry
tldr: Bayesian optimization of Si3N4 membranes yields sail-shaped trampoline resonators that lower frequency while maintaining high Q-mass product. These designs promise improved sensitivity for optomechanical accelerometers.
significance: This work enables more sensitive inertial sensors by combining low-frequency operation with high mechanical quality.

title: VideoRAE: Taming Video Foundation Models for Generative Modeling via Representation Autoencoders
headline: VideoRAE: Enhancing Video Generation with Representation Autoencoders
tldr: VideoRAE bridges the gap between 3D-VAEs and Video Foundation Models by learning richer latent spaces that capture semantic and spatio-temporal structure. The approach improves generative performance while leveraging the strong video understanding of models like V-JEPA 2 and VideoMAEv2.
significance: It enables more semantically coherent video generation by combining the strengths of autoencoders and foundation models.

title: Leveraging unlabelled data for generalizable neural population decoding
headline: Unsupervised Pretraining Boosts Neural Decoding Across Sessions for Brain-Computer Interfaces
tldr: This study introduces a self-supervised framework that leverages unlabeled spike data to pretrain neural decoders, achieving state‑of‑the‑art performance across multiple recording sessions. The approach enables generalizable, real‑time decoding without the need for extensive labeled datasets.
significance: By unlocking the vast amounts of unlabeled neural recordings, the method dramatically improves decoder robustness and accelerates deployment of brain‑computer interfaces.

title: Benchmarking trigonometric continuous-variable gate primitives with trapped ions
headline: Benchmarking Trigonometric CV Gate Primitives for Quantum Computing on Trapped Ions
tldr: This study evaluates the performance of trigonometric continuous-variable gate primitives implemented on trapped-ion systems, comparing their fidelity and resource requirements against conventional gate sets. The results demonstrate that these periodic-function-based operations can achieve comparable or superior performance with reduced circuit depth, offering a promising direction for hybrid CV-discrete quantum processors.
significance: The work establishes a benchmark for trigonometric CV gates, highlighting their potential to streamline hybrid quantum algorithms and accelerate the development of scalable quantum processors.

title: Investigating anomalous microwave emission near G107.2+5.20 in Ku-band with the Green Bank Telescope
headline: Mapping Anomalous Microwave Emission Near G107.2+5.20 with the Green Bank Telescope
tldr: The study uses the GBT Ku-band receiver to map 30 GHz-peaking anomalous microwave emission (AME) around G107.2+5.20, revealing how local environmental conditions shape the AME spectral energy distribution. These spatially resolved observations help constrain spinning dust models and improve our understanding of AME mechanisms.
significance: By providing the first high-resolution AME map of this region, the work advances the characterization of spinning dust emission and its dependence on interstellar environments.

title: Building Shor's Algorithm in Lean: An Agentic Formalization of Quantum Attacks on RSA-2048 and P-256
headline: Formalizing Shor’s Algorithm in Lean: Quantum Attacks on RSA-2048 and P‑256
tldr: Large language models now aid formal theorem proving in Lean, enabling the construction of a fully verified Shor’s algorithm. The work demonstrates how agentic systems can search, reuse, and extend formal libraries to expose new quantum‑cryptanalysis results.
significance: This research bridges AI‑assisted formal verification and quantum cryptanalysis, providing a reproducible, machine‑checked foundation for future quantum‑attack proofs.

title: Linear Independent Component Analysis via Optimal Transport
headline: Optimal Transport Enhances Linear ICA for Improved Signal Separation in Remote Sensing
tldr: This study introduces an optimal transport framework for linear ICA, enabling more accurate recovery of independent source signals. By leveraging Wasserstein distance, the method outperforms traditional contrast functions in both synthetic and real-world remote sensing datasets.
significance: It provides a scalable, theoretically grounded alternative to conventional ICA, potentially benefiting diverse applications from medical imaging to environmental monitoring.

title: A modular state-space model of human perception, cognition, and decision dynamics
headline: Modular State-Space Model Captures Human Perception, Cognition, Decision Dynamics
tldr: This paper introduces a modular state-space framework that models human behavior as a perception‑cognition‑decision pipeline, enabling interpretable latent dynamics. The approach bridges black‑box predictors and psychologically grounded models, offering a mathematically analyzable tool for adaptive systems.
significance: By providing transparent internal states, the model enhances the design of human‑centered adaptive systems and facilitates rigorous analysis of behavioral dynamics.

title: Renormalizing a three-flavor lattice calculation of the two-photon contribution to $K_L\toμ^+μ^-$
headline: Renormalizing Three-Flavor Lattice QCD to Compute Two-Photon Contribution in KL→μ⁺μ⁻
tldr: The paper presents a renormalization scheme for a three-flavor lattice QCD calculation of the long-distance two-photon contribution to the rare decay KL→μ⁺μ⁻. By neglecting 1/mc² terms, the authors achieve a more accurate Standard Model prediction for this decay.
significance: It refines the theoretical prediction of a key rare kaon decay, improving tests of the Standard Model and constraints on new physics.

title: From Pixels to States: Rethinking Interactive World Models as Game Engines
headline: Video Generative Models as Next-Gen Game Engines: From Pixels to Interactive States
tldr: The paper explores how video generative models can predict future observations conditioned on player actions, offering a data-driven alternative to traditional game engines. It discusses challenges and opportunities in achieving coherent, real-time interactivity in virtual worlds.
significance: It proposes a paradigm shift toward AI-driven game engines that can generate realistic, responsive environments without handcrafted assets.

title: VisualRepair: Dynamic Tool Calling and Region Focusing for Visual Software Issue Repair
headline: VisualRepair: Leveraging Visual Cues for Automated Bug Fixing with LLMs
tldr: VisualRepair introduces a dynamic tool‑calling framework that focuses on relevant regions of bug screenshots to guide large language models in generating accurate software fixes. By integrating multimodal inputs, it improves repair precision and reduces the need for manual debugging.
significance: This approach bridges the gap between visual bug reports and automated repair, enabling more efficient and accurate fixes in GUI‑heavy applications.

title: Quantum Transport and Apparent Work Function Distributions of Atomic Contacts via a 3D-Printed High-Vacuum Platform
headline: Low-Cost 3D-Printed Vacuum Platform Enables Quantum Transport Measurements at Room Temperature
tldr: We built a low-cost, 3D-printed high-vacuum platform that integrates a mechanically controllable break-junction and a custom logarithmic amplifier. Using copper, we resolved the 1G0 conductance quantum in both high vacuum and anhydrous glycerol, demonstrating the platform’s versatility.
significance: This platform democratizes quantum transport experiments by providing a cost-effective, modular solution that works in diverse environments.

title: An exactly solvable macroscopic fluctuation theory of single-file diffusion
headline: Exact Solution of Macroscopic Fluctuation Theory for Single-File Brownian Hard Rods
tldr: The paper presents an exact solution to the macroscopic fluctuation theory of single-file diffusion modeled by Brownian hard rods. This breakthrough provides analytical expressions for density fluctuations and transport properties, offering a benchmark for future studies.
significance: It resolves a long-standing challenge in statistical physics by delivering closed-form predictions for a class of interacting particle systems.

title: MetaPerch: Learning from metadata for bioacoustics foundation models
headline: MetaPerch: Harnessing Metadata from Citizen Science Platforms to Boost Bioacoustic Foundation Models
tldr: MetaPerch demonstrates that incorporating readily available recording metadata can significantly improve species detection in bioacoustic foundation models. By leveraging data from platforms such as Xeno‑Canto, the approach sets a new benchmark for performance while remaining open‑source.
significance: This work unlocks untapped metadata to elevate bioacoustic models, offering a scalable, open‑source solution for ecological monitoring.

title: Do AI Agents Know When a Task Is Simple? Toward Complexity-Aware Reasoning and Execution
headline: AI Agents Lacking Complexity Awareness: A Call for Scope Estimation in LLM Workflows
tldr: Large language model agents often over‑engage tasks, re‑reading data unnecessarily. Introducing task‑aware execution‑scope estimation can streamline workflows and reduce computational waste.
significance: Understanding task complexity enables more efficient, scalable AI automation across engineering and informatics domains.

title: DenseReward: Dense Reward Learning via Failure Synthesis for Robotic Manipulation
headline: DenseReward: Learning Dense Rewards by Synthesizing Failures for Robot Manipulation
tldr: The paper introduces DenseReward, a method that generates synthetic failure data to train vision-language reward models that provide dense feedback for robotic manipulation tasks. It demonstrates that dense rewards improve sample efficiency and policy performance compared to sparse signals.
significance: This work bridges the gap between sparse reinforcement learning and dense, fine-grained reward signals, enabling more efficient learning for complex manipulation tasks.

title: The Seriality Gap in Video Diffusion Models
headline: Seriality Gap: Video Diffusion Models Fail on Long Causal Chains in Multi-Ball Dynamics
tldr: We show that standard bidirectional video diffusion struggles to predict outcomes when the causal chain lengthens, even with more denoising steps. A single-ball control without interactions confirms that the degradation is due to serial interactions, not model capacity.
significance: This highlights a fundamental limitation of current diffusion-based video models for tasks requiring long-range temporal reasoning.

title: On the Detectability of Volcanic Exo-Ios That May Fuel Auroras on Super-Jupiters
headline: Detecting Volcanic Exo-Ios: New Clues to Auroras on Super-Jupiters in Exoplanetary Systems
tldr: Recent radio burst observations hint that volcanic moons around giant exoplanets could supply plasma for auroras. Detecting such exo-Ios would confirm a key mechanism powering these distant auroral storms.
significance: Identifying volcanic exo-Ios would illuminate the origins of exoplanetary auroras and refine models of star–planet interactions.

title: TerraZero: Procedural Driving Simulation for Zero-Demonstration Self-Play at Scale
headline: TerraZero: Fast, Realistic Procedural Driving Simulator for Large‑Scale Autonomous Self‑Play
tldr: TerraZero introduces a high‑speed procedural driving simulator built in C, enabling reinforcement learning agents to train at scale without relying on logged data. By grounding simulations in real‑world map structures and generating diverse scenarios, it addresses safety‑critical edge cases that traditional datasets miss.
significance: TerraZero provides a scalable, realistic platform that bridges the gap between simulation fidelity and training efficiency for autonomous driving research.

title: PalmClaw: A Native On-Device Agent Framework for Mobile Phones
headline: PalmClaw Brings Native LLM Agent Framework to Mobile Phones for On-Device Automation
tldr: PalmClaw introduces a lightweight, on-device LLM agent framework that can execute multi-step tasks using native mobile tools. It enables real-time, privacy-preserving automation directly on smartphones without server dependency.
significance: By enabling LLM agents to run natively on mobile devices, PalmClaw expands AI capabilities to ubiquitous, data-rich environments while preserving user privacy.

title: Non-intrusive MEMS microphone sensing of acoustic field state in resonant acoustic levitators
headline: Non-Intrusive MEMS Microphone Sensing for Real-Time Acoustic Field Monitoring in Levitation Systems
tldr: A MEMS microphone array can non-invasively measure the acoustic field inside resonant levitators, enabling closed-loop adjustment of transducer-reflector distance. This approach improves stability across temperature shifts, object insertion, and alignment errors.
significance: Accurate, real-time acoustic field sensing is critical for reliable operation of compact levitation devices, reducing power consumption and enhancing precision.

title: Inclusive P-wave Quarkonium Decay Widths from Lattice QCD and pNRQCD
headline: Lattice QCD and pNRQCD Compute P-wave Quarkonium Decay Widths for Light Hadrons
tldr: A new framework merges lattice QCD with strongly-coupled pNRQCD to calculate inclusive P-wave quarkonium decay widths into light hadrons. The method isolates nonperturbative effects to the derivative of the wavefunction, enabling precise predictions.
significance: This advances first-principles predictions for heavy quarkonium decays, bridging lattice simulations and effective field theory.

title: Simulating the convection in red super-giant stars: wobbling jets in common envelope evolution
headline: Simulating Convection in Red Supergiants Reveals Wobbling Jets During Common Envelope Evolution
tldr: Researchers built a 3‑D red supergiant model that mimics nuclear energy production and photospheric emission to study the stochastic angular momentum of mass accreted by a companion during common envelope evolution. The simulations show that the accreted mass can produce wobbling jets, offering new insights into binary interactions.
significance: Understanding jet dynamics in common envelope evolution is crucial for predicting the outcomes of massive binary systems and their role as supernova progenitors.

title: Software Supply Chains are Dead: Use-Case-Oriented Regeneration
headline: Software Supply Chains Are Dead: Rethinking Dependencies with Generative AI
tldr: Software supply chain attacks have made external dependencies costly, while generative AI reduces the need for them. This article proposes a use‑case‑oriented approach to regenerate software components locally.
significance: It challenges the traditional build‑vs‑reuse paradigm, urging developers to adopt AI‑driven local implementation.

title: Gamma-ray Modes in a Transitional Pulsar
headline: Gamma‑Ray Modes Revealed in Transitional Pulsar PSR J1023+0038
tldr: Scientists discovered that the transitional millisecond pulsar PSR J1023+0038 emits distinct gamma‑ray signatures correlated with its X‑ray mode switching. By stacking Fermi‑LAT observations aligned with simultaneous X‑ray data, they revealed two separate gamma‑ray modes, confirming a link between accretion and rotation power.
significance: This finding provides the first direct evidence that gamma‑ray emission in transitional pulsars is modulated by their accretion state, offering new insights into the interplay between accretion disks and magnetospheric processes.
